steps.title: Solve for the needed coefficient
steps.content: For an equation in $x$ to have no solutions, the $x$-coefficients must match but the constants must not match. Expanding the right side gives $$7x+4=ax+ab.$$ Matching the $x$-coefficients gives $a=7$, so statement I must be true.

steps.title: Find the excluded value of b
steps.content: With $a=7$, the equation becomes $7x+4=7x+7b$. If $4=7b$, the equation would have infinitely many solutions instead of no solutions. Therefore no solutions require $$7b\neq4\Rightarrow b\neq\frac{4}{7}.$$

steps.title: Translate the condition
steps.content: The condition is exactly $$a=7\quad\text{and}\quad b\neq\frac{4}{7}.$$ That means $a$ must equal $7$, while $b$ only has to avoid one value.

steps.title: Select the answer
steps.content: Statement I is required. Statement II is not required because $b$ can be any value except $\frac{4}{7}$. Statement III is required. Therefore only I and III must be true, which is choice D.
